skill.name: web-ai-prompt-api
skill.description: Implementation reference for Chrome's built-in Prompt API with Gemini Nano, covering sessions, availability, streaming, structured output, multimodal input, and Chrome extension usage.
skill.summary: Build browser AI features with Chrome's Prompt API and local Gemini Nano.
skill.author: AJV009

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, crypto, browser storage, and network patterns, but review found the file is a markdown implementation guide with JavaScript examples, not executable skill code. The command and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from inline code fences and wording. A low privacy concern remains because the guide demonstrates storing conversation history in localStorage and shows granting Prompt API access to a cross-origin iframe.

low_findings.title: Conversation History Persistence Example
low_findings.description: The guide demonstrates restoring sessions from localStorage and saving user and assistant prompts. This is not executable skill behavior, but copied examples could persist sensitive conversation content in browser storage without explicit retention controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The localStorage usage is directly shown in a documentation example and is repeated as a context preservation practice. Risk is limited because the skill itself is markdown and does not execute the code.

low_findings.title: Cross-Origin Permission Policy Example
low_findings.description: The guide shows granting language-model access to a cross-origin iframe. This is a legitimate browser API example, but copied usage could expose local model capability to embedded third-party content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The iframe permission pattern is present and semantically relevant to browser capability exposure. The example uses a placeholder domain and does not perform an actual network request.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Static Execution Alerts in Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The external command and weak cryptography alerts map to JavaScript snippets, markdown text, browser URLs, and Prompt API examples. No runnable shell script, package hook, or command invocation is present in the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a markdown reference document with fenced JavaScript, HTML, and JSON examples. I found no executable wrapper or instruction that runs shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Hardcoded URL Alert
dangerous_patterns.description: The hardcoded URL is a placeholder iframe source used to explain Permission Policy behavior. It is not contacted by the skill and does not exfiltrate data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside an HTML documentation snippet for iframe configuration. There is no surrounding executable code that performs a network request.

content.value_statement: Browser AI development requires careful handling of model availability, sessions, streaming, and structured output. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a compact reference for implementing Chrome Prompt API features with Gemini Nano.
actual_capabilities: Explains Prompt API availability checks and model creation options.
Shows session setup with temperature, topK, initial prompts, and abort signals.
Documents non-streaming and streaming prompt calls for browser AI responses.
Covers JSON Schema response constraints for predictable structured output.
Describes multimodal input, session cloning, local restoration, and extension support.
limitations: It is a reference guide, not a runnable library or application.
It depends on Chrome versions and origin trial support that may change.
It focuses on desktop Chrome and does not support mobile browser deployments.
It does not include full production security patterns for storing user conversations.

use_cases.title: Prototype Browser AI Features
use_cases.description: Use the guide to add local summarization, classification, or chat behavior to a web page.

use_cases.title: Design Chrome Extension AI Workflows
use_cases.description: Reference extension availability, permissions, and session patterns before implementing local AI features.

use_cases.title: Plan Privacy-Sensitive AI Processing
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether on-device Prompt API behavior can reduce server-side processing for user content.

prompt_templates.title: Check API Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain how I should check Chrome Prompt API availability before creating a session.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Streaming Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a streaming Prompt API flow with cancellation and user-visible progress states.

prompt_templates.title: Add Structured Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Prompt API response constraint for extracting three fields from page text.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Extension Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Chrome extension plan for Prompt API sessions, storage, permissions, and fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Explain how to start using the Prompt API in a web app.
output_examples.output: A concise setup checklist covering Chrome version, hardware requirements, availability checks, model download status, and session creation.

output_examples.input: Design a structured output flow for page classification.
output_examples.output: A practical plan that uses response constraints, validates the model output, handles unsupported browsers, and warns about quota limits.

output_examples.input: Review a Chrome extension idea that stores chat history.
output_examples.output: A risk-focused implementation review that covers permission policy, local storage retention, user consent, and session cleanup.

best_practices: Check availability with the same options used for session creation.
Use streaming and abort signals for long responses and responsive interfaces.
Treat stored prompts as user data and define clear retention behavior.
anti_patterns: Assuming the model is available without checking download and hardware status.
Persisting conversation history without user consent or a deletion path.
Granting iframe access to the language model without a clear trust boundary.

faq.question: Does this skill run code?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown reference for implementing Chrome Prompt API features.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is prepared for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does the Prompt API work on mobile Chrome?
faq.answer: The guide states that mobile platforms are not supported.

faq.question: Can it help with structured model output?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains response constraints with JSON Schema for predictable responses.

faq.question: Does it cover Chrome extensions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes notes about extension support and origin trial permissions.

faq.question: What should developers review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review browser support, storage retention, iframe permissions, quota handling, and fallback behavior.
